NAME

Register fork handlers for common module.

110                                                                              {
111   pthread_atfork(NULL, NULL, asciichat_common_atfork_child);
112 }








void
  asciichat_shared_destroy (void )


Clean up all shared library subsystems. Performs comprehensive
    cleanup of all subsystems initialized by asciichat_shared_init(). All
    cleanup functions are idempotent (safe to call multiple times), so this can
    be called explicitly and also via atexit() without issues.




Cleanup order is carefully chosen to be the reverse of
    initialization, ensuring dependencies are respected (e.g., timer cleanup
    before memory reporting, memory reporting before errno cleanup).




Note


This function is safe to call even if init failed or
  wasn't called.



All subsystem cleanup functions must remain idempotent.




Definition at line 191 of file common.c.


191                                     {
192   // Guard against double cleanup (can be called explicitly + via atexit)
193   static bool shutdown_done = false;
194   if (shutdown_done) {
195     return;
196   }
197   shutdown_done = true;
198 
199   symbol_cache_destroy();
200 
201   // Cleanup in reverse order of initialization (LIFO)
202   // This ensures dependencies are properly handled
203 
204   // 0. Stop background threads first (before any cleanup that might use them)
205   // Terminal resize detection thread (Windows only, checks if active)
206   terminal_stop_resize_detection();
207 
208 #ifndef NDEBUG
209   // Lock debug thread - must join before any lock cleanup
210   debug_sync_cleanup_thread();
211 
212   // Clean up all remaining mutex stacks before memory report
213   mutex_stack_cleanup();
214 
215   // Clean up current thread's allocations
216   debug_sync_final_cleanup();
217 
218   // Memory debug thread - prints memory report (must be last)
219 #if defined(DEBUG_MEMORY)
220   debug_memory_thread_cleanup();
221 #endif
222 
223   // Lock debug system - set initialized=false so mutex_lock uses mutex_lock_impl directly
224   // This must happen after thread cleanup but before any subsystem that uses mutex_lock
225   debug_sync_destroy();
226 
227   // Atomic debug cleanup
228   debug_atomic_shutdown();
229 #endif
230 
231   // 1. Terminal screen - cleanup frame buffer
232   terminal_screen_cleanup();
233 
234   // 2. SIMD caches - cleanup CPU-specific caches
235   simd_caches_destroy_all();
236 
237   // 3. Discovery service strings cache - cleanup session string cache
238   acds_strings_destroy();
239 
240   // 4. Logging - in correct order (end first, then begin)
241   log_shutdown_end();
242   log_shutdown_begin();
243 
244   // 5. Known hosts - cleanup authentication state
245   known_hosts_destroy();
246 
247   // 6. Options state - cleanup RCU-based options
248   options_state_destroy();
249 
250   // 7. Buffer pool - cleanup global buffer pool
251   buffer_pool_cleanup_global();
252 
253   // 8. Platform cleanup - restores terminal, cleans up platform resources
254   // (includes symbol cache cleanup on Windows)
255   platform_destroy();
256 
257   // 9. Keyboard - restore terminal settings (redundant with platform_destroy but safe)
258   keyboard_destroy();
259 
260   // 10. Timer system - cleanup timers (may still log!)
261   timer_system_destroy();
262 
263   // 11. Error context cleanup
264   asciichat_errno_destroy();
265 
266   // 12. Logging cleanup - free log format buffers and system state
267   log_destroy();
268   log_system_destroy();
269 
270   // 13. Mutex stack cleanup - must be before memory report so stacks are freed
271 #ifndef NDEBUG
272   mutex_stack_cleanup();
273   // Final cleanup: free the main thread's debug allocations
274   debug_sync_final_cleanup();
275 #endif
276 
277   // 14. Memory stats (debug builds only) - runs with colors still available
278   //     Note: PCRE2 singletons are cleaned up later via atexit handler (runs after this function)
279   //     This ensures code can still use PCRE2 during normal program shutdown
280   //     Note: debug_memory_report() is also called manually during shutdown in server_main()
281   //     The function is safe to call multiple times with polling-based locking
282 #if defined(USE_MIMALLOC_DEBUG) && !defined(NDEBUG)
283   print_mimalloc_stats();
284 #endif
285 
286 #if defined(DEBUG_MEMORY) && !defined(NDEBUG)
287   debug_memory_report();
288 #endif
289 
290   // 15. Color cleanup - free compiled ANSI strings (AFTER memory report)
291   log_cleanup_colors();
292   colorscheme_destroy();
293 
294   // 16. Clean up binary path cache explicitly
295   // Note: This is also called by platform_destroy() via atexit(), but it's idempotent
296   platform_cleanup_binary_path_cache();
297 
298   // 17. Clean up RCU-based options state
299   options_state_destroy();
300 
301   // 18. Clean up errno context (allocated strings, backtrace symbols)
302   asciichat_errno_destroy();
303 
304 #ifndef NDEBUG
305   // 19. Named registry - cleanup all registered thread names and debug entries
306   // Must come BEFORE PCRE2 cleanup since named_destroy() uses path normalization which needs PCRE2
307   named_destroy();
308 #endif
309 
310   // 20. PCRE2 - cleanup all regex singletons together (after named_destroy)
311   asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all();
312 
313   // 21. timer resolution restore
314   platform_restore_timer_resolution(); // Restore timer resolution (no-op on POSIX)
315 
316   // 22. Clean up SIMD caches
317   simd_caches_destroy_all();
318 
319   // 23. Clean up symbol cache
320   // This must be called BEFORE log_destroy() as symbol_cache_destroy() uses log_debug()
321   // Safe to call even if atexit() runs - it's idempotent (checks g_symbol_cache_initialized)
322   // Also called via platform_destroy() atexit handler, but explicit call ensures proper ordering
323   symbol_cache_destroy();
324 
325   // 24. Clean up global buffer pool (explicitly, as atexit may not run on Ctrl-C)
326   // Note: This is also registered with atexit(), but calling it explicitly is safe (idempotent)
327   // Safe to call even if atexit() runs - it checks g_global_buffer_pool and sets it to NULL
328   buffer_pool_cleanup_global();
329 
330 #ifndef NDEBUG
331   // 25. Symbol cache cleanup
332   symbol_cache_destroy();
333 #endif
334 }






References acds_strings_destroy(),
    asciichat_errno_destroy(), asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all(),
    buffer_pool_cleanup_global(), colorscheme_destroy(),
    debug_atomic_shutdown(), debug_memory_report(),
    debug_memory_thread_cleanup(), debug_sync_cleanup_thread(),
    debug_sync_destroy(), debug_sync_final_cleanup(),
    known_hosts_destroy(), log_cleanup_colors(),
    log_destroy(), log_shutdown_begin(),
    log_shutdown_end(), log_system_destroy(),
    mutex_stack_cleanup(), named_destroy(),
    options_state_destroy(), platform_cleanup_binary_path_cache(),
    platform_destroy(), simd_caches_destroy_all(),
    symbol_cache_destroy(), terminal_screen_cleanup(), and
    timer_system_destroy().


Referenced by client_main(), and main().






asciichat_error_t
  asciichat_shared_init (const char * log_file, bool is_client)


Definition at line 115 of file common.c.


115                                                                               {
116   // Initialize shared subsystems BEFORE options_init()
117   // This allows options_init() to use properly configured logging with colors
118   //
119   // NOTE: This function is called BEFORE options_init(), so we can't use GET_OPTION()
120   // Palette config and quiet mode will be applied after options_init() is called
121 
122   // Only register initialization tracking once
123   if (!g_shared_initialized) {
124     // 1. TIMER SYSTEM - Initialize first so timing is available for everything else
125     if (!timer_system_init()) {
126       FATAL(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Failed to initialize timer system");
127     }
128 
129     // KEYBOARD SYSTEM must be initialized BEFORE logging since keyboard_init() calls log_info()
130     // But we need to initialize logging AFTER timer_system_init() and BEFORE keyboard_init()
131     // So we move keyboard_init() to AFTER log_init()
132 
133 #ifndef NDEBUG
134     if (named_init() != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
135       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Failed to initialize named.");
136     }
137     debug_memory_ensure_init();
138     symbol_cache_init();
139 #endif
140 
141     // 2. LOGGING - Initialize system state first, then user-level logging
142     // Initialize logging system internal state (terminal caps, colors)
143     log_system_init();
144 
145     // Initialize with provided log file
146     // Force stderr for client-like modes when stdout is piped to keep stdout clean
147     bool force_stderr = is_client && terminal_is_piped_output();
148 
149     // Use LOG_DEBUG by default; will be reconfigured after options_init()
150     log_init(log_file, LOG_DEBUG, force_stderr, false /* don't use_mmap */);
151 
152     // 2. KEYBOARD SYSTEM - Initialize keyboard input AFTER logging (may fail if stdin/tty not available)
153     // Failure is not fatal - keyboard_read_nonblocking() safely handles uninitialized state
154     keyboard_init();
155 
156     // 3. PLATFORM - Initialize platform-specific functionality (Winsock, etc)
157     if (platform_init() != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
158       FATAL(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Failed to initialize platform");
159     }
160 
161     // 4. BUFFER POOL - Initialize global shared buffer pool
162     buffer_pool_init_global();
163 
164     // Mark that we've initialized (prevents re-registration of subsystems)
165     g_shared_initialized = true;
166   }
167 
168   // NOTE: This library function does NOT register atexit() handlers.
169   // Library code should not own the process lifecycle. The application
170   // is responsible for calling atexit(asciichat_shared_destroy) if it
171   // wants automatic cleanup on normal exit.
172 
173   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
174 }






References buffer_pool_init_global(),
    debug_memory_ensure_init(), log_init(),
    log_system_init(), named_init(), platform_init(),
    symbol_cache_init(), terminal_is_piped_output(), and
    timer_system_init().


Referenced by main().






bool shutdown_is_requested (void )


Definition at line 73 of file common.c.


73                                  {
74   shutdown_check_fn callback = (shutdown_check_fn)atomic_ptr_load(&g_shutdown_callback);
75   if (callback == NULL) {
76     return false; // No callback registered, assume not shutting down
77   }
78   return callback();
79 }






References atomic_ptr_load().


Referenced by log_plain_msg(),
    log_plain_stderr_msg(), log_plain_stderr_nonewline_msg(), and
    splash_wait_for_animation().
